calendar_note: The Leonorian year has 365 days divided into 10 months. The current year is Year 0 of the Resurgent Era.

timeline.description: A cataclysmic event of unknown origin that struck the central Pangea. The exact cause is disputed by every culture that has studied it. The central continent was scorched, twisted, and warped. The laws of nature were rewritten. The Badlands were born. Many believe it killed or fundamentally changed every race that existed at the time.
consequences: Creation of The Badlands (central scorched zone)
Birth or transformation of the Archons
Mutation of the Elementally Afflicted races
Partial corruption and maddening of the Wildmen
The Forgotten Kingdom sealed off from the surface world
Norium deposits formed throughout The Eternal Winds
timeline.known_unknowns: No reliable records exist from before The Great Death. All pre-Death history is mythological.

timeline.description: A millennium of post-apocalyptic chaos following The Great Death. Survival was the only law. No organized states existed. Races fought for scarce resources in the altered landscape. The Archons began consolidating their power. The Elementally Afflicted scattered to the margins.
consequences: Population collapse across all races
Formation of survival-based tribal structures
First emergence of the Archons as a dominant force
Wildmen settle in the fringes of the Dark Forests and Steppes

timeline.description: The rise of disparate martial powers. A brutal period of constant border shifts and tribal dominance. The Common Folk first emerged as a coherent racial identity during this era, rising from the survivors of the Warworld. The first proto-states formed and collapsed in rapid succession.
consequences: First Common Folk kingdoms appear and fall
Archons build early city-states in The Sanctuary Lands
Stone Folk retreat fully into mountains and the upper Forgotten Kingdom
Oakpeople culture takes root in the deep Sanctuary Lands
First Wildmen chiefdoms emerge in the Dark Forests

timeline.description: A brief but historically significant era of partial unification. A figure or lineage known only as 'Canda' unified or dominated large portions of the continent. The nature of Canda is disputed — some cultures venerate Canda as a Common Folk emperor, others as an Archon scholar-king. The era ended violently.
consequences: First continent-spanning trade routes established
Shared calendar system adopted (later reformed)
Cultural exchange between Archons and Common Folk
Collapse into the Golden Age of the Archons after Canda's fall

timeline.era: The Golden Age of the Archons
timeline.description: The peak of the elder races. The Secluded, The Greys, The Ice Archons, and The Dark Ones all flourished during this period. High magic and architectural wonders defined the era. The Greys built their great Spire-Cities. The Secluded expanded their forest territories. The Dark Ones consolidated control of the Forgotten Kingdom. Common Folk civilizations existed under Archon overlordship or in peripheral regions.
consequences: Great architectural wonders constructed (many now in ruins in the Badlands and Midlands)
The eight major schools of Materium fully codified
The Forgotten Kingdom mapped and zoned (The Light, The Well, The Fallen)
Common Folk cultures grew in the Midlands, slowly gaining independence
Ice Archons retreated fully to The Eternal Winds — first complete isolation

timeline.era: The Age of the Eight Kingdoms
timeline.description: The splintering of Archon power into eight major mortal-led sovereign entities. Common Folk kingdoms rose to prominence as the Archons' grip on political power weakened. The Wildmen Hordes pressed from the Dark Forests. Step Folk confederacies formed in the Blinding Lands and Gleam Havens. The 28 cultures of the current era trace their lineages to this period.
consequences: Eight dominant kingdoms divide the continent
Common Folk become politically dominant in The Midlands
Step Folk confederacies recognized as sovereign entities
Wildmen Horde raids reshape northern and western borders
Dark Ones increasingly isolated as The Forgotten Kingdom becomes politically autonomous

timeline.era: The Tyranny of Irin the Sage
timeline.description: A dark century where Irin the Sage, a Dread Knight of the Forgotten Kingdom, enslaved much of Leonoria. Irin was either a fallen Ancient or an exceptionally powerful Ashen Halfbreed — records are destroyed or suppressed. Irin enforced a global reign of terror through an army of bound undead and corrupted champions. Was eventually brought down by a coalition, but the exact nature of the coalition's victory is also disputed.
consequences: Mass destruction of historical records (many pre-Irin histories lost)
Widespread undead presence established in the Dark Forests and Badlands
The Forgotten Kingdom's political structure permanently altered
Several Midland and Sanctuary Lands cultures erased or merged
The Gatekeepers order founded to monitor and control access to the Forgotten Kingdom

timeline.description: The collapse of Irin's empire triggered a long, multi-generational conflict. Different factions fought to fill the power vacuum. The conflict is named for a key battle fought at The Well zone of the Forgotten Kingdom, where the fate of the underground world was decided. The war eventually exhausted all major powers and forced the negotiated peace that created the current political map.
consequences: Current borders and the 28-culture system established
Gatekeeper order gains formal political recognition
Undead presence reduced but not eliminated from Dark Forests and Badlands
The Outer Steppes emerge as a no-man's land buffer zone
Step Folk cultures gain full sovereign recognition in Blinding Lands and Gleam Havens
Stone Folk of The Well gain autonomy within the Forgotten Kingdom

timeline.description: The current era, characterized by the stabilization of the 28 core cultures and their respective states. Trade routes are reopening. Old ruins are being explored. The Badlands remain impassable to most, but trade arteries through its edges are profitable. Tensions simmer between The Midlands hegemons and the Wildmen hordes. The Forgotten Kingdom is largely self-governing and mysterious to outsiders.
current_tensions: Midlands expansion pressure on Dark Forest tribes
Wildmen raids along the Sanctuary Lands borders
Dark Ones' Um-Syndicate expanding influence through trade networks
Increased undead activity in the Badlands fringe (unexplained)
Ice Archons sighted further south than recorded history — reason unknown

leonorian_calendar.birth_month_signs: Each month is governed by a Birth Mother Sign. See birthmothersigns.json.
